items.q: Which mood follows 'quiero que' (I want that)?
items.why: Students use indicative after que. Expressions of wish, emotion, doubt, or impersonal expressions trigger SUBJUNCTIVE: 'Quiero que VENGAS' (not 'que vienes'). Different subjects require que + subjunctive.

items.q: When do you use the imperfect rather than the preterite?
items.correct: For ongoing, habitual, or descriptive past actions.
items.misconception: When the action happened a long time ago.
items.random_wrong: When you're unsure of the exact date.
items.why: Students choose tense by recency. The distinction is ASPECTUAL: preterite = completed/single ('comí' I ate); imperfect = ongoing/habitual ('comía' I used to eat / I was eating).

items.q: Which preposition expresses 'for' as duration (e.g., 'for two hours')?
items.why: Students choose para because it's the default 'for'. POR is used for duration/extent of time. Para 'for two hours' would suggest a deadline ('to be ready BY two hours' time').
Correct answer: Pencil. “Pen” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: La regla. “El boli” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many classroom items are listed in this lesson?
items.why: The real figure is 6. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
